title: Kendal Ozzel
content:
{{Top}} {{Update|[[Tarkin (novel)|Tarkin]], [[Darth Vader (2015) 25|''Darth Vader'' (2015) 25]], [[Doctor Aphra (2016) 4|''Doctor Aphra'' (2016) 4]], [[Star Wars Book XI: Hope Dies]], [[Star Wars Book XIV: Rebels and Rogues]], [[Forces of Destiny—Leia|Star Wars: Forces of Destiny—Leia]], [[From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back]], [[Darth Vader (2020) 6]], [[Imperial Officers and Agents]], [[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Ozzel.jpg]] |name=Kendal Ozzel |homeworld=[[Carida]]''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]'' |birth= |death=[[3 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], in which Kendal Ozzel was [[Death|killed]], to [[3 ABY]]. ''[[Executor]]'', [[Hoth system]]{{Film|V}} |species=[[Human]]{{SWE|characters|admiral-ozzel|Admiral Ozzel}} |gender=[[Gender|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=1.79 [[meter]]s {{C|5[[Foot|ft]] 9in}}''[[Star Wars: Character Encyclopedia, Updated and Expanded Edition]]'' |mass= |hair=[[Color|Blond]], later gray |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families=[[Ozzel family]]{{SWCT|set=Base Series 1|cardname=Admiral Ozzel - Imperial Officer}} |parents=[[Kendal Ozzel's mother]]{{StoryCite|story=Kendal|book=From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back}} |partners=[[Kendal Ozzel's fiancée]] |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic]]''[[Star Wars Character Encyclopedia: Updated and Expanded]]'' **[[Republic Navy]] *[[Galactic Empire]] **[[Joint Chiefs of the Imperial Military|Joint Chiefs]][[Tarkin (novel)|''Tarkin'']] **[[Imperial Navy's officer corps]] **[[Death Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|He is as clumsy as he is stupid.|Darth Vader, talking to General Veers about Kendal Ozzel|}} '''Kendal Ozzel''' was a [[human]] [[Gender|male]] [[officer]] who served the [[Galactic Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire]] in turn. [[Reproduction|Born]] into a wealthy [[Ozzel family|family]] from the [[planet]] [[Carida]] prior to the [[Proclamation of the New Order|rise]] of the Empire, he served in the [[Republic Navy]] as a [[Republic officer|commissioned officer]] during the [[Clone Wars]]—a civil war fought in [[the galaxy]] during the [[Republic Era|last years]] of the Republic. After the war, his [[Imperial Military|military]] [[Imperial officer|career]] would continue under the [[New Order|new regime]] established by [[Galactic Emperor|Emperor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]]. Ozzel gave up everything for the Empire, even giving up his chance at a simple life with his [[Kendal Ozzel's fiancée|fiancée]]. As a [[Fleet Admiral|fleet admiral]], Ozzel was the commanding officer of the ''[[Executor]]''—an [[Imperial Navy]] [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] and the [[flagship]] of the [[Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]]—at the [[time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War]]. In the [[Standard year|year]] [[3 ABY]], he oversaw the initial [[Battle of Hoth|attack]] on [[Hoth]] after the Empire discovered the location of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Echo Base|secret base]], but was [[Force choke|summarily executed]] by way of [[the Force]] as a result of Vader's displeasure in the admiral's fundamentally incompetent tactics. As he was choked, Ozzel realized he had given his life over to authoritarians, though it was too late to change all he had done. Following Ozzel's [[death]], his [[Military rank|rank]] and command were awarded to his [[captain]], [[Firmus Piett]], by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith|Dark Lord]]. ==Biography== ===Early service=== {{Quote|Join the rebel forces.|Kendal Ozzel's fiancée offers him a life against the Empire|}} A [[Gender|male]] [[human]] [[Reproduction|born]] into a wealthy [[Ozzel family|family]] from [[Carida]], Kendal Ozzel attended multiple [[Core Worlds|Core World]] institutes in his youth. Eventually, he graduated and became a [[Captain of the Line]]. However, his superiors did not see him fit to serve as an effective field [[commander]], and he was relegated to teaching positions for a while at the [[Carida Academy|Academy of Carida]].{{EncyclopediaCite|Imperial Officers and Agents}} Ozzel's military career was [[Standard decade|decades]]-long. He served for a [[time]] in the [[Republic Navy]] during the [[Clone Wars]]. When Ozzel was still in his younger [[Standard year|years]], he decided he would serve in the Republic's successor state,{{Film|III}} the [[Galactic Empire]], but he also became engaged to be married to a [[Kendal Ozzel's fiancée|woman]]. Thus, Ozzel could have had a simpler life and be married on his [[homeworld]] of Carida, with his friends and family in attendance. However, one night, his fiancée offered him a chance to join the rebellion against the Empire. Having already decided his loyalty was to the Empire, Ozzel and his would be wife cried and held each other for the night before parting ways forever the next morning. Ozzel thus served in the [[Imperial Navy]]. In [[14 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the events of [[Tarkin (novel)|''Tarkin'']] to [[14 BBY]]. he held the rank of [[Rear Admiral]], and was a member of the [[Joint Chiefs of the Imperial Military|Joint Chiefs]]. ===Admiralship=== {{Quote|Admiral Ozzel. When will the ''Executor'' be ready?''"
"''The…the…''Executor'' suffered minor damage in its hijacking, but first estimates project it's relatively limited in scope. We should be ready for it's launch in a month.''"
"''You have two weeks.''"
"''That's impossible, Lord Vader! The workforce will—''"
"''You sound suspiciously like General Tagge.''"
[glances at Tagge's body] "''The ''Executor will be ready, Lord Vader.|Vader and Ozzel|}} [[File:VaderKillsTagge-2015DarthVader25.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Ozzel witnesses Tagge's death at Vader's hand, a fate he would one day share.]] Following the [[Battle of Yavin|destruction]] of the First [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]], he had become an [[Admiral]]. He personally issued a communique to the Imperial Starfleet ordering the fleet to enter battle readiness.''[[Smuggler's Run: A Han Solo & Chewbacca Adventure]]'' After this occurred the [[Cylo Hijacking|Hijacking of the ''Executor'']], in which the [[Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] dispatched the [[scientist]] [[Cylo]], who had attempted to steal the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] ''[[Executor]]''. The [[Super Star Destroyer|SSD]] was originally designed to be given to [[Grand General]] [[Cassio Tagge]], however, [[Galactic Emperor|Emperor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]] demoted him to [[General]], and gave the ''Executor'' to Vader, due to Tagge's trust of Cylo. Vader [[The Force|Force]] [[Force choke|choked]] Tagge on his new [[command ship]]'s [[Bridge (starship)|bridge]] in front of Ozzel.[[Darth Vader (2015) 25|''Darth Vader'' (2015) 25]] Watching the incident caused Ozzel to feel a secret thrill. Vader asked him when the ''Executor'' would be ready, and gave him two [[Standard week|weeks]], after Ozzel replied he needed a [[Standard month|month]]. After [[Captain]] [[Magna Tolvan]] [[Raid at the Great Temple|failed]] to capture [[Chelli Lona Aphra]], who was investigating the [[Great Temple]], Ozzel contacted the captain, and warned her that Vader would be less forgiving of failure than Cassio Tagge, her former commanding officer.[[Doctor Aphra (2016) 4|''Doctor Aphra'' (2016) 4]] ===Weeds in a storm=== {{Quote|At the least, we need more strength–Your Death Squadron must be brought to bear immediately. It's ludicrous for us to keep it–''"
"''"Ludicrous?"''"
"''Lord Vader. I… I… I meant no disrespect''"
"''What you meant is of no interest to me.|Ozzel and Vader discussing the war against the Rebellion|{{StoryCite|story=Shu-Torun Lives|book=Star Wars (2015) 50}}}} Sometime later, Ozzel along with General [[Maximilian Veers|Veers]], met with Vader at [[Fortress Vader|his castle]] on [[Mustafar]]. As the Sith Lord looked out over the world's landscape, the admiral relayed how the Death Star's destruction had galvanized the rebellion. Resources for the Empire's construction projects were being delayed and what Rebel forces there were too scattered and well hidden to make effective progress against. Ozzel went on to suggest that a fleet carrying out a world-by-world bombardment would serve as a suitable deterrent and told Vader that the Death Squadron had to be the force that applying the bombardment. Just as he was saying it was ludicrous to not use the fleet, Vader interrupted him by questioning the admiral's usage of the word ludicrous. After apologizing, Ozzel went on to question how they could eliminate the Rebels, comparing them to vermin. Veers then spoke up and contradicted the Admiral, instead comparing them to weeds. Angered, Ozzel kept his gaze on Veers as the general explained how weeds survive storms and how the Rebellion would continue to survive Imperial attacks as they were flexible and not as sturdy as a tree. Turning his attention back to Vader, Ozzel addressed the Sith Lord after he showed interest in what Veers had said. ===Searching for the Rebel base=== {{Quote|We have thousands of probe droids searching the galaxy. I want proof, not leads!|Admiral Ozzel|}} [[File:YouSuckPiett-ESBHD.jpg|thumb|260px|Ozzel and Piett preparing for the attack on Hoth.]] Ozzel eventually reached the rank of [[Fleet Admiral]] and became the commanding [[Imperial officer|officer]] of the [[Death Squadron]], an armada of Imperial [[Starship|vessels]]{{SWE|technology|imperialstardestroyer|Imperial Star Destroyer}} tasked with locating the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Alliance's]] hidden main base in the aftermath of the Battle of [[Yavin Prime|Yavin]], though the [[fleet]] was under the ''de facto'' control of Vader. In his capacity as admiral of the fleet, Ozzel led [[Death]] [[Squadron]] from Vader's [[flagship]], the ''Executor''. As he had been assigned to oversee the Empire's search for the Rebel headquarters, Ozzel felt that he was the perfect being to command the massive [[warship]] during the [[Hunting|hunt]]. During his time as the senior [[officer]] aboard the ''Executor'', Ozzel took time to personally congratulate [[Lieutenant Commander]] [[Ciena Ree]], a young officer whose service had been outstanding for the past two [[Standard year|years]]. After commending Ree for her dedication, the admiral implied that she might obtain a promotion to the rank of Commander soon enough.''[[Lost Stars]]'' The Empire dispatched thousands of [[probe droid]]s throughout [[the galaxy]] to participate in the search for the Rebel base, [[XJ9-CS14|one]] of which discovered signs of an [[Echo Base|occupied settlement]] on the [[Water|ice]] [[planet]] [[Hoth]]. Before being destroyed by Rebel Captain [[Han Solo]], the [[droid]] relayed its data to the ''Executor'' in the [[Juris sector]],''[[The Odyssey of Star Wars: An Epic Poem]]'' where it was reviewed by Admiral Ozzel, his subordinate, Captain [[Firmus Piett]], and General Veers of the [[Imperial Army]]. Piett cited the data as their best lead in the search, but Ozzel dismissed the information as lacking definitive proof of any Rebel presence. Nevertheless, Vader overruled him and ordered the fleet to make haste to the [[Hoth system]] to deal with the Rebels headquartered there. ===Final thoughts=== {{Quote|Lord Vader, the fleet has moved out of lightspeed, and we're preparing to…''"
[Vader force chokes Ozzel] "''You have failed me for the last time, Admiral. Captain Piett? Make ready to land our troops beyond the energy field and deploy the fleet so nothing gets off the system. You are in command now, Admiral Piett.|Ozzel's final report to Vader|audio=YouHaveFailedMeForTheLastTimeAdmiral.flac|}} As Vader withdrew to his [[Darth Vader's meditation chamber|private quarters]], Ozzel assumed command of the fleet's maneuvering and pre-battle strategy. Believing he could surprise and overwhelm the Rebels, Ozzel opted to have the [[Star Destroyer]]s of Death Squadron emerge from [[hyperspace]] directly above Hoth. The Alliance detected their arrival immediately, but the tactic did not shock them as Ozzel had planned, for the presence of the probe droid had made them suspect that the Empire had discovered their base. Before Death Squadron could initiate an [[orbital bombardment]], the Rebels activated the base's [[Deflector shield|shield]], which was powerful enough to deflect any [[laser]] barrage unleashed by the Imperial warships; even the full firepower of the ''Executor'' wouldn't be able to breach it. [[File:Ozzel croaks.jpg|thumb|left|300x300px|Vader personally saw to it that Ozzel's tactical blunder at Hoth was his last mistake.]] General Veers reported this development to Vader, who voiced his disapproval at the admiral's tactical blunder as they would now be forced to deploy [[troop]]s and equipment into a costly ground battle rather than simply annihilate the Rebels with a simple orbital bombardment from the fleet. The [[Sith]] Lord opened a [[Viewscreen|channel]] to Ozzel on the ''Executor''{{'s}} bridge and, before an oblivious Ozzel could report on the situation, began [[Telekinesis|telekinetically]] strangling the admiral in full view of Captain Piett and others. While Ozzel initially assumed the choke was Vader teaching him a well-deserved lesson, Vader cited the tactical decision as Ozzel's final failure and executed the officer, simultaneously ordering a blockade around Hoth and promoting Piett to take his place as admiral, bestowing the title at the exact moment Ozzel's still-twitching corpse hit the floor. As he died, Ozzel thought back on his life, coming to the realization that he had willingly given over his entire life to authoritarians. He regretted his choices and wished he had been a rebel spy and shared a life with his fiancée. His last thoughts were remembering a memory from his childhood, one of him running up a mountain to come home for dinner. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|And if we made a complaint about Admiral Ozzel's death, sir?''"
"''I think we both know what would happen, Commander.|Tian Karmiya and Canonhaus|{{StoryCite|story=The Final Order|book=From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back}}}} The Empire went on to win the [[Battle of Hoth]] despite Ozzel's actions. While the battle was considered to be the Alliance's worst defeat, however, it wasn't a crushing one. Ozzel had inadvertently given time to the rebels to evacuate when he brought the fleet out of hyperspace too close to Hoth. [[Historian]]s of the [[Galactic Civil War]] concluded that if it hadn't been for their defeat on Hoth, the Rebel Alliance may not have risked everything the [[4 ABY|following year]] in the decisive [[Battle of Endor]].[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2016)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] ==Personality and traits== Standing 1.75 [[meter]]s tall, Admiral Kendal Ozzel was a smug and arrogant man who fancied himself the obvious choice to command the ''Executor''. In his position as the ranking officer of Darth Vader's Death Squadron, he often tested the Sith Lord's patience by challenging his orders and making foolish tactical decisions, as his personal opinions always differed from those of Vader.{{ForceCollection|Kendal Ozzel|star=3}} Ozzel's subordinate, the ambitious Captain Piett, had nothing but contempt for the admiral, whom he considered incompetent.{{SWE|characters|admiral-piett|Admiral Piett}} Ultimately, Ozzel's personality, combined with his blunder in the opening stages of the Battle of Hoth, persuaded Vader to immediately—and permanently—remove him from command, and replace him with Piett. A man of science and warfare, Ozzel did not think much of Darth Vader's use of the Force, which the admiral regarded as vulgar hocus-pocus. Despite that, Ozzel was still terrified of Vader, like most other officers.''[[The Empire Strikes Back: So You Want to Be a Jedi?]]'' Even so, he respected Vader, viewing the Force chokes the Sith Lord used as a way to make him and other subordinates learn lessons. Ambitious young captains like Firmus Piett annoyed him; in particular, Ozzel thought Piett was entirely too enthusiastic, as if everything he said might have proved sufficient for a promotion. In his final moments, Ozzel regretted his life and wished he had joined the rebellion with his fiancée. ==Behind the scenes== Admiral Ozzel appeared in the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], the second installment of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[original trilogy]]. He was portrayed by the Scottish actor [[Michael Sheard]]. Having seen the original [[1977]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] with his eldest son in its premiere and enjoyed it, Sheard didn't audition for the role of Ozzel. Instead, he was sent the ''Empire'' script by the casting director. By the time he finished his work on the [[1979]] film [[Wikipedia:Escape to Athena|''Escape to Athena'']], his name was up on the casting's board for the role of Ozzel. [[Irvin Kershner]] directed all of Ozzel's scenes, assuring Sheard that his death scene was perfect in just one take.{{WebCite|url=http://michaelsheard.com:80/interviews/johnj.htm|text=John Jagwani Interviews Michael Sheard|work=[http://michaelsheard.com MichaelSheard.com]|archivedate=20060108003425}} In [[2004]], it was erroneously reported by a [[Lucasfilm]] producer that a young Ozzel would appear in the [[2005]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], the final installment of the ''Star Wars'' [[prequel trilogy]], when asked on television, though the executive in reality refused to confirm or deny Ozzel's potential appearance. Already not a fan of the prequels, Sheard debunked the report in an interview, remarking that he was old enough to play Ozzel's father instead.{{WebCite|url=http://michaelsheard.com:80/interviews/tbone.htm|text=Michael Sheard Interview With T-bone|work=[http://michaelsheard.com MichaelSheard.com]|archivedate=20060108004119}} The finished film didn't include Ozzel in any capacity at all. In the reference book ''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]'', Ozzel's first name was mistakenly spelled as "Kendel" instead of "Kendal."{{Twitter|pablohidalgo|status/710136823515881472|[[Pablo Hidalgo]]|quote=@LelalMekha It should be Kendal. Unfortunately, I think this stubborn typo made it's way into the Character Encyclopedia too.|archivefile=File:Kendel_vs_Kendal.png}} ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= *[[Tarkin (novel)|''Tarkin'']] {{Ab|Tarkin (audiobook)}} *''[[Lost Stars]]'' {{Ab|Lost Stars (audiobook)}} *[[Lost Stars (webcomic)|''Lost Stars'' webcomic]] *''[[Star Wars Treasury: The Original Trilogy]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Original Trilogy – A Graphic Novel]]'' *{{StoryCite|story=An Incident Report|book=From a Certain Point of View|audiobook=1}} {{C|Erroneous mention}} *''[[Smuggler's Run: A Han Solo & Chewbacca Adventure]]'' {{Ab|Smuggler's Run: A Han Solo & Chewbacca Adventure (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars Adventures: Smuggler's Run 1|''Star Wars Adventures: Smuggler's Run'' 1]] {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: Commander]]'' *[[Darth Vader (2015) 25|''Darth Vader'' (2015) 25]] *[[Doctor Aphra (2016) 4|''Doctor Aphra'' (2016) 4]] {{Hologram}} *{{StoryCite|story=Shu-Torun Lives|book=Star Wars (2015) 50}} *[[Star Wars (2015) 50|''Star Wars'' (2015) 50]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 51|''Star Wars'' (2015) 51]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 52|''Star Wars'' (2015) 52]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 53|''Star Wars'' (2015) 53]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 54|''Star Wars'' (2015) 54]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 55|''Star Wars'' (2015) 55]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 68|''Star Wars'' (2015) 68]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 69|''Star Wars'' (2015) 69]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 70|''Star Wars'' (2015) 70]] *[[Star Wars (2015) 74|''Star Wars'' (2015) 74]] {{Voice}} *[[Star Wars (2015) 75|''Star Wars'' (2015) 75]] *[[Doctor Aphra (2016) 40|''Doctor Aphra'' (2016) 40]] {{Mo}} *''[[Forces of Destiny—Leia]]'' *{{Film|V}} {{1st}} *''[[The Empire Strikes Back: So You Want to Be a Jedi?]]'' {{Ab|The Empire Strikes Back: So You Want to Be a Jedi?
